items.prompt: What is the vowel harmony class of 'kert' (garden)?
items.explanation: The vowels e in kert are front unrounded; suffixes use front variants (e.g. -ben, not -ban).

items.prompt: What is the vowel harmony class of 'ház' (house)?
items.explanation: á is a back vowel; suffixes take back variants (e.g. -ban, -nak).

items.prompt: Choose the correct form: kert + inessive (in the garden)
items.explanation: Front unrounded stem kert takes -ben: kertben.

items.prompt: Choose the correct form: föld + superessive (on the ground)
items.explanation: Mixed vowels: ö triggers front rounded harmony; superessive is -ön here: földön.

items.prompt: What is the vowel harmony class of 'tükör' (mirror)?
items.explanation: ü and ö are front rounded vowels; suffixes use ö/ü variants (e.g. -höz, -ből).
